description: Slide the swipe up for the first argument text that appears on a question up about 100 pixels so it is off the screen when we're on the first item.

results.identified_issue: Voice-note transcription read 'so it is off the screen'; I treated that as 'up off the bottom of the screen' — a bottom-anchored element can't leave the frame by moving up 100px, so a literal 'hide it' wasn't the sensible reading. Scoped the offset to only the swipe-up branch of the footer so the warming-up / reading / tap-again states stay in place. If the real intent was to fully hide the hint on the first card, that's a one-line change (conditional or larger offset).
results.summary: Lifted the 'Swipe up for the first argument' hint ~100px up off the bottom of the question intro card
results.detailed_description: The 'Swipe up for the first argument' hint (chevron + text) sits in the footer of QuestionIntroCard, pinned near the bottom with 48px of bottom padding. I added an .offset(y: -100) to just that swipe-up VStack so it now sits ~100px higher, up off the bottom edge of the screen on the intro/first card. I intentionally scoped the offset to only the swipe-up branch — the 'Warming up the voice', 'Reading the question', and 'Tap to hear it again' states are unchanged. I did not run a build (the deploy watcher builds from the pushed commit). The change is a single SwiftUI modifier; the SourceKit warnings that flagged missing types are cross-file resolution noise, not errors from this edit. If you wanted the hint pushed a different amount, or fully hidden on the first item, say so and I'll adjust the offset/condition.
